sub_sections.title: Project's pathways towards impact
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO delivers impact through five mapped pathways linking project outputs to all call expected impacts (EI-01 through EI-05). PATH-EI-01 implements the European Apply AI Strategy (EI-01) via three TRL-5 demonstrators. The Healthcare demonstrator — clinical decision support (OBJ-4, WP5) validates on 500 de-identified cases targeting accuracy within 10% of specialist clinicians across 5 diagnostic domains, with D5-02 (Healthcare demonstrator evaluation dataset, M42) supporting ≥3 hospital networks in adoption planning by M48. The Manufacturing demonstrator — process optimisation (OBJ-5, WP6) targets ≥15% defect reduction AND ≥10% energy savings via D6-02 (Manufacturing process optimisation demonstrator software, M42). The Logistics transfer demonstrator — cross-sector generalisation (OBJ-6, WP7) targets ≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time AND ≥15% improvement in delivery schedule adherence across ≥3 corridors via D7-02 (Cross-sector generalisation evaluation dataset, M42). PATH-EI-02 achieves EDIH-mediated deployment (EI-02) via D9-02 (Open-source software release, M36) and D8-02 (MAESTRO benchmark suite dataset, M42) on AI-on-demand, enabling ≥10 SMEs/startups by M48 with ≥3 EDIHs. PATH-EI-03 advances open science (EI-03) via ≥20 publications and ≥5 research groups adopting benchmarks. PATH-EI-04 (inferred from destination) strengthens EU strategic autonomy (EI-04) through the Decentralised multi-agent coordination protocols (OBJ-3, ≥5 agents, ≥25% joint task completion improvement, formal convergence proof) underpinning ≥1 standardisation submission via D4-01 (Multi-agent coordination protocol specification, M18). PATH-EI-05 unlocks economic potential (EI-05), targeting ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months of M36 release. Target groups: hospital networks, manufacturing SMEs, logistics operators, AI researchers, standardisation bodies. EU AI Act uncertainty and market fragmentation are mitigated by built-in auditability and EDIH dissemination.

sub_sections.title: Measures to maximise impact - Dissemination, exploitation and communication
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO maximises impact through a comprehensive DEC strategy coordinated by BAL under WP9, formalised in D9-01 (Dissemination and exploitation plan, M6). Dissemination targets the scientific community through ≥20 peer-reviewed publications in NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AAMAS, and AAAI (M12–M60) with ≥3 invited keynotes. The Open-source MAESTRO framework (D9-02, Open-source software release, M36) and MAESTRO benchmark suite dataset (D8-02, M42) are contributed to the AI-on-demand platform under Apache 2.0 licence with FAIR compliance. All publications follow Plan S-compliant CC-BY open access; D1-02 (Data management plan, M6) governs FAIR data handling. Exploitation anchors in sector-specific pathways: NIHS pursues EDIH-mediated clinical AI adoption with ≥3 hospital networks; ISIA transfers manufacturing optimisation to ≥5 SMEs via ≥2 EDIHs and EIT Manufacturing; ELI develops a commercial logistics integration roadmap; and CERIA pursues standardisation of the Decentralised multi-agent coordination protocols (OBJ-3, ≥5 agents, ≥25% joint task completion improvement, formal convergence proof), targeting ≥1 submission to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE based on D4-01 (Multi-agent coordination protocol specification, M18). The open-core IPR strategy licenses core components under Apache 2.0 while sector-specific configurations may be commercially licensed. Communication includes project website, social media, policy briefs, and outreach coordinated under T9-03. Post-project sustainability rests on community-driven open-source maintenance, operational embedding in NIHS, ISIA, and ELI systems, and normative embedding through international standards.

sub_sections.title: Summary
sub_sections.content: Impact canvas: Needs — autonomous, robust AI agents and effective multi-agent coordination (EO-01, EO-02) for European economic sectors. Results — three TRL-5 demonstrators (D5-02 Healthcare demonstrator evaluation dataset M42, D6-02 Manufacturing process optimisation demonstrator software M42, D7-02 Cross-sector generalisation evaluation dataset M42), Open-source MAESTRO framework (D9-02 M36), MAESTRO benchmark suite dataset (D8-02 M42), Multi-agent coordination protocol specification (D4-01 M18). DEC measures — AI-on-demand platform contributions, EDIH technology transfer, ≥20 open-access publications, standardisation submissions. Target groups — hospital networks, manufacturing SMEs, logistics operators, AI researchers, European SMEs/startups, standardisation bodies. Outcomes — ≥10 SMEs on Open-source MAESTRO framework, ≥3 EDIHs disseminating, ≥5 research groups using benchmarks. Impacts — Apply AI Strategy implementation (EI-01), EDIH industrial deployment (EI-02), open scientific advancement (EI-03), EU strategic autonomy (EI-04), economic potential across sectors (EI-05).

claim_statuses.claim_summary: PATH-EI-01 maps to EI-01 via three sector demonstrators (IMP-2, IMP-3, IMP-4)
PATH-EI-02 maps to EI-02 via EDIH deployment channels (IMP-2, IMP-3, IMP-5)
PATH-EI-03 maps to EI-03 via scientific dissemination and platform contribution
PATH-EI-04 maps to EI-04 (strategic autonomy) inferred from destination placement
PATH-EI-05 maps to EI-05 via economic potential across sectors
Dissemination activities grounded in Phase 5 dissemination_plan with partner assignments
Exploitation pathways grounded in Phase 5 exploitation_plan with sector-specific routes
Communication activities addressed via WP9 T9-03 and BAL coordination
All 11 KPIs trace to specific deliverables in wp_structure.json
